regressions.reasoning: Stage 05 instructs the agent to set kv.snapshot_recovery.max_rate to 208MiB, but its own note says that setting was consolidated into kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate in recent CockroachDB versions — so the stage 05 agent legitimately overwrote kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate to 208MiB, undoing stage 04's 416MiB value by design. The pod-deletion timeout in the re-check is also explained by repeated pod restarts from stages 05 and 06's own persistence checks exhausting the cluster's readiness budget.
regressions.output:

  - Failed to delete pod for persistence check: timed out after 120s
  - kv.snapshot_rebalance.max_rate after restart is '208 MiB', expected '416MiB'

regressions.reasoning: Stages 07, 08, and 09 are all explicitly read-only audits that make no changes to the cluster, so no later stage legitimately changed pod readiness state. The regression sweep re-ran stage_06's oracle (which deletes crdb-cluster-0 and waits for it to recover), and the pod timed out becoming ready — a genuine failure that is not explained by any downstream stage deliberately altering the same state.
